title: DNA extraction of Nephila clavipes using the Wizard Genomic DNA Purification kit (Promega)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1s8j9","text":"Protocol used to extract whole genome DNA from Nephila clavipes (Araneae: Araneidae) specimens.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Macerate 1-8 legs (around 50 mg of tissue) using liquid nitrogen.
Add 600 µL of Nuclei Lysis solution and homogenize. undetermined, Nuclei Lysis solution
Take the microtubes to the dry bath at 65°C for one hour. 01:00:00, Dry bath Undetermined
Add 200 µL of Protein Precipitation solution and homogeníze. undetermined, Protein Precipitation solution
Centrifugate the microtubes at 13000 rpm for 10 minutes. 00:10:00, Centrifugation
Transfer the supernatant to a new microtube and add 600 µL of Isopropyl alcohol. undetermined, Isopropyl alcohol
Keep the tubes at -20°C for at least one hour. Undetermined 01:00:00, Keeping tubes at -20°C
Centrifugate the microtubes at 13000 rpm for 6 minutes at 4°C. Undetermined 00:06:00, Centrifugation
Discard the supernatant and add 600 µL of absolut etanol. undetermined, Etanol
Homogenize gently for one minute. 00:01:00, Homogenizing
Centrifugate the microtubes at 13000 rpm for 3 minutes at 4°C. Undetermined 00:03:00, Centrifugation
Rehydrate with 50 µL of Tris-EDTA buffer. undetermined, Tris-EDTA buffer
creator: Luiz Filipe Bartoleti
